search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae nausea vomiting telehealth Issue 2 assessment red flags investigations referral
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic gastroenteritis, obstruction, pregnancy, metabolic or neurological diagnosis
automatic antiemetic, fluid, laboratory, imaging, referral or discharge advice
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Vomiting has many possible causes and the workflow remains undifferentiated.
All investigations, treatments, medicines and dispositions remain clinician-confirmed.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: People with nausea or vomiting; the DHA applicability section does not state a precise age range and pregnancy, paediatric, postoperative and immunocompromised presentations require added review.
setting_applicability: Dubai telehealth evidence with partial applicability to in-person emergency assessment.
UAE_applicability: UAE-direct DHA source applies to licensed Dubai telehealth, while local emergency, laboratory, imaging, pregnancy, paediatric and referral pathways govern.
recency_verification: DHA Issue 2 effective 2024-04-21 with revision due 2029-02-21 was reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: Issue 2 remains current on the DHA portal.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact DHA assessment section supports onset, duration, severity or functional impact, associated symptoms, relevant negatives and patient concerns for nausea or vomiting without inferring the cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Specifies distinction between nausea, vomiting, retching, and regurgitation and records duration, frequency, associated symptoms, intake, and likely causes.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag section supports clinician risk context, manual vital-sign and focused assessment, severity, appearance and time-critical findings only when assessed.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists severe abdominal pain, gastrointestinal bleeding, neurological symptoms, chest pain, weight loss, dehydration, altered state, and metabolic concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact investigation section supports recording existing results or imaging only when already ordered, performed or reviewed; it does not recommend testing.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: States that testing is history-directed and lists glucose, blood count, inflammatory markers, electrolytes, stool culture, and urinalysis.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact referral section supports clinician-decided escalation, plan, completed specialist discussion and arranged handover without generating referral or treatment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists urgent referral features including haematemesis, neurological symptoms, chest pain, acute abdominal pain, gastrointestinal bleeding, and immunosuppression.

unresolved_source_gaps: Episode count, contents, hydration, intake, pregnancy, exposures, abdominal or neurological examination, test values, diagnosis, treatment, medication and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
